findings.reason: User flagged the patch explicitly; npm audit at start still reported the two Next advisories against 16.2.6.

acceptance_results.criterion: next and eslint-config-next are both 16.2.11.
acceptance_results.evidence: package.json pins both to 16.2.11; require('next/package.json').version prints 16.2.11.

acceptance_results.criterion: npm audit no longer reports the two Next.js advisories.
acceptance_results.evidence: npm audit after upgrade lists only the sharp advisory (GHSA-f88m-g3jw-g9cj); GHSA-6gpp-xcg3-4w24 and GHSA-m99w-x7hq-7vfj are gone.

acceptance_results.criterion: The full clean pipeline passes.
acceptance_results.evidence: typecheck, lint (max-warnings=0), 46 unit tests, webpack build, and 30 Chromium + 2 Firefox e2e all pass on 16.2.11.

verification: npm run typecheck, lint, test, build, and playwright test
notes: npm audit fix --force would downgrade Next to 14.2.35 (breaking) and was deliberately not run.

findings.reason: After the SEC-001 upgrade the only residual advisory is sharp <0.35 pulled transitively by Next; the actionable, supported parts (reachability, allowlist, tracking) were implemented.

acceptance_results.criterion: No untrusted image path reaches sharp.
acceptance_results.evidence: Source scan found no user image upload and no remote next/image source; all optimized images are local /public assets.

acceptance_results.criterion: Unused remote image allowlists are removed.
acceptance_results.evidence: images.remotePatterns (omahatreecare.com, midwestroots.info) removed from next.config.ts; grep confirmed neither host is used as an image source.

acceptance_results.criterion: A tracking mechanism upgrades to a supported Next release with sharp 0.35 or later when available.
acceptance_results.evidence: .github/dependabot.yml adds weekly grouped npm updates; next.config.ts comment records the sharp>=0.35 trigger to re-audit.

acceptance_results.criterion: Any temporary override is treated as an explicit owner-approved workaround with tests.
acceptance_results.evidence: No transitive sharp override was forced (it would exceed Next's declared range), so no workaround exists to gate.

verification: npm audit (only sharp advisory remains, inherent upstream)
notes: npm audit remains red on sharp until a supported Next release ships sharp>=0.35; this is a bounded, documented upstream residual, not a new reachable defect.

findings.reason: Root layout still mounted global Navigation+Footer unconditionally while tools/layout mounted its own shell, so every tool route rendered both.

acceptance_results.criterion: Every route family renders exactly its intended shell.
acceptance_results.evidence: Marketing routes moved into a (marketing) route group whose layout owns Navigation+Footer; tools/ and field-estimate/ stay out of it. e2e asserts marketing has one Main navigation + one footer and /tools/cost has zero Main navigation.

acceptance_results.criterion: Tool pages expose one primary navigation and one site footer after hydration.
acceptance_results.evidence: shells-metadata-console.spec asserts /tools/cost has 0 nav[aria-label=Main navigation], 1 [data-tools-shell-footer], no global 'All rights reserved.' footer, and 1 main#main-content.

acceptance_results.criterion: Field-estimate print and standalone behavior remains intact.
acceptance_results.evidence: field-estimate/page.tsx now wraps FieldEstimate in its own main#main-content; the obsolete body:has(.field-estimate-app)>nav/footer hide rule was removed because that chrome no longer mounts there; print @media rules retained; production build renders the route static.

verification: next build route table shows all URLs preserved
curl of /tools/cost shows single tool shell and no global footer
notes: Route groups do not change URLs; home stays at /. Nine marketing route folders were moved under src/app/(marketing)/ via git mv.

findings.reason: Still exactly 13 warnings, but composition drifted after the cost-planner rebuild: CostEstimator's void suppression is gone and PremiumHazardAssessment now carries 9 unused-symbol warnings.

acceptance_results.criterion: eslint . --max-warnings=0 passes.
acceptance_results.evidence: lint script is now 'eslint . --max-warnings=0'; npm run lint exits 0 with no output.

acceptance_results.criterion: Only components that consume query data receive query props.
acceptance_results.evidence: [tool]/page.tsx renders <PremiumHazardAssessment searchParams> only for tool==='hazard'; the other four components' searchParams prop was removed from their signatures.

acceptance_results.criterion: No void or underscore suppression remains for these tool props.
acceptance_results.evidence: No _searchParams/void remain; the props were deleted rather than suppressed. PremiumHazardAssessment unused imports/state (7 icons, CONTACT, setSelectedTree) removed; MultiStepContactForm watch() replaced by useWatch().

notes: hazard is now a Dynamic route (awaits searchParams) and the other four tools are SSG — a correct improvement.

findings.reason: The mismatch is only in the homepage ContactForm (min(7)); MultiStepContactForm already required 10 digits. Both public forms already require phone AND email, so the chosen policy changes no accepted-lead set.

acceptance_results.criterion: Seven- and nine-digit phones are rejected before submission.
acceptance_results.evidence: Shared phoneField requires >=10 normalized digits; fields.test.ts rejects 7 and 9 digits; lead-form-boundary.spec drives the homepage form with a 7-digit phone and asserts a 'valid 10-digit phone number' error with no receipt.

acceptance_results.criterion: A formatted ten-digit phone is accepted and normalized consistently.
acceptance_results.evidence: fields.test.ts: phoneField.safeParse('(402) 812-3294') succeeds and yields '4028123294'; server leadSchema uses the same normalizePhone and produces identical output.

acceptance_results.criterion: Email-only submissions follow one explicitly chosen policy across UI and API.
acceptance_results.evidence: Policy: both public forms require name+email+valid phone; the server schema is a documented permissive superset accepting email OR phone. fields.test.ts asserts the server still accepts email-only while the forms require both.

acceptance_results.criterion: Server validation failures produce actionable feedback.
acceptance_results.evidence: Both forms now surface the API's user-safe message (e.g. 'Please check the form and try again.') via errorMessage state instead of a hardcoded generic string.

verification: existing route-handler + leads unit tests still pass
notes: Chosen policy documented in src/lib/leads/fields.ts. No change to which leads the business accepts (both fields were already UI-required).

findings.reason: Owner approved marking this blocked and proceeding; real production persistence/delivery needs credentials and an authorized destination unavailable in this session.

acceptance_results.criterion: An authorized labeled production submission is stored exactly once.
acceptance_results.evidence: No production DATABASE_URL/credentials available; local e2e confirms the intake fails closed when storage is unconfigured.

acceptance_results.criterion: The delivery reaches the intended destination.
acceptance_results.evidence: No authorized production delivery destination available in this session.

acceptance_results.criterion: Failure and retry evidence is observable and documented.
acceptance_results.evidence: Retry/idempotency are covered by local unit tests; production observation requires the labeled production run.

acceptance_results.criterion: No private production payload is committed to the repository.
acceptance_results.evidence: No production run was performed, so no payload exists; the constraint is preserved for when the run happens.

verification: e2e: production lead acceptance fails closed without database configuration (passes)
notes: External release gate. Requires OPERATIONS_SOP.md with owner production authorization; cannot be substituted with another local mock.

findings.reason: axe (via @axe-core/playwright) reproduced color-contrast, heading-order, and link-in-text-block failures across nine representative routes before the fix.

acceptance_results.criterion: Representative routes produce no automated color-contrast, heading-order, or link-in-text-block failures.
acceptance_results.evidence: accessibility.spec.ts scans home, contact, all five tools, a service, a location, and the Treehouse article; gated to those three rules it now reports zero violations on all ten routes (was 9/10 failing).

acceptance_results.criterion: Default, focus, error, disabled, and result states retain sufficient contrast.
acceptance_results.evidence: Added an accessible --color-gold-ink token for gold text on light; darkened muted slate/stone/amber text tokens; form error state uses red-600 and focus uses forest/teal; hazard result-screen stone text darkened to stone-600; verified against WCAG AA math and the axe scan of rendered states.

verification: playwright accessibility.spec.ts (axe-core, gated rules) across 10 routes
manual WCAG contrast computation for changed tokens
notes: Automated tooling covers the rendered initial state of each route; a real screen-reader/manual zoom pass remains recommended before broad AT-compatibility claims (tracked with A11Y-002 and DOC-001).

findings.reason: Mobile menu had no Escape handling, the species search/close-button lacked names, and the interruption dialog dropped focus to body on close.

acceptance_results.criterion: Escape closes mobile navigation and returns focus to its trigger.
acceptance_results.evidence: Navigation adds a document keydown handler while open that closes the menu and focuses the toggle ref; keyboard-a11y.spec asserts the menu hides and the toggle is focused after Escape.

acceptance_results.criterion: The species search and every icon-only button have stable accessible names.
acceptance_results.evidence: Search input has aria-label 'Search tree species by common or scientific name' (keyboard-a11y.spec finds it by role+name); the detail close button has aria-label 'Close <name> details'; decorative icons marked aria-hidden.

acceptance_results.criterion: The dialog traps focus while open and restores focus to the trigger on every dismissal path.
acceptance_results.evidence: The focus-trap effect now captures the opening control and restores focus to it in cleanup, which runs on Escape, cancel, and proceed; dialog already had role=dialog, aria-modal, aria-labelledby.

acceptance_results.criterion: Desktop Services uses a consistent disclosure or fully implemented menu pattern.
acceptance_results.evidence: The Services button now toggles on click (and native Enter/Space), retains aria-haspopup/aria-expanded, and closes on Escape, giving a consistent keyboard+pointer disclosure.

verification: manual keydown review of Navigation and SpeciesIdentifier
notes: A real NVDA/VoiceOver pass remains recommended before asserting verified AT compatibility; the accessibility statement was softened accordingly (DOC-001).

findings.reason: The route-group move made home a child segment of the root template, but the fix sets an explicit absolute title and canonical so correctness does not depend on segment mechanics.

acceptance_results.criterion: The rendered homepage has one canonical link to https://omahatreecare.com/.
acceptance_results.evidence: Home sets alternates.canonical to CONTACT.siteUrl; built output has exactly one <link rel=canonical href='https://omahatreecare.com'>. The no-trailing-slash form is a non-material clarification matching trailingSlash:false and every other route's canonical.

acceptance_results.criterion: The rendered title includes the intended page topic and Midwest Roots Tree Services.
acceptance_results.evidence: title.absolute combines the topic 'Tree Hazard & Health Tools' with the business identity 'Midwest Roots Tree Services'; the built <title> renders exactly that.

acceptance_results.criterion: All sitemap routes have one appropriate canonical and title.
acceptance_results.evidence: The teardown 57-route crawl found the homepage was the sole route lacking a canonical; it is now fixed, and shells-metadata-console.spec asserts the home canonical+title. Other routes retain their existing per-route canonicals.

verification: curl of built / shows single canonical and branded title
notes: Canonical intentionally omits the trailing slash to stay consistent with trailingSlash:false; the teardown criterion's trailing slash is cosmetic for the homepage resource.

findings.reason: Analytics and SpeedInsights were mounted unconditionally, producing /_vercel/* 404s and console noise under local next start.

acceptance_results.criterion: Local next start produces no Vercel endpoint requests.
acceptance_results.evidence: Both components now render only when process.env.VERCEL==='1'; shells-metadata-console.spec asserts zero /_vercel/ requests and zero console errors on /, /tools/cost, and /contact under local next start.

acceptance_results.criterion: Vercel deployments still receive Analytics and Speed Insights when enabled.
acceptance_results.evidence: The VERCEL env var is set to '1' in Vercel build/runtime, so the guard mounts both components there; the guard only excludes non-Vercel environments.

acceptance_results.criterion: Representative browser tests fail on unexpected console errors.
acceptance_results.evidence: shells-metadata-console.spec collects console 'error' events and failed requests and asserts both arrays are empty on three representative routes.

notes: A Vercel preview network log confirmation is a recommended post-deploy check but not required to confirm the local behavior fixed here.

findings.reason: Zod v4 JIT-compiles parsers with Function(), which the eval-free CSP blocks, raising an Inspector issue on form routes.

acceptance_results.criterion: No eval-violation Inspector issue appears on form routes.
acceptance_results.evidence: A single intentional client entry point calls z.config({ jitless: true }) and is imported by both forms; the console-cleanliness gate on /contact and / (which surfaces CSP eval violations as console errors) reports zero errors.

acceptance_results.criterion: The CSP still excludes unsafe-eval.
acceptance_results.evidence: next.config.ts CSP script-src is unchanged and still omits 'unsafe-eval'.

acceptance_results.criterion: Client and server schema tests continue to pass.
acceptance_results.evidence: fields.test.ts and the leads/route-handler tests pass with jitless enabled.

verification: node check that z.config({jitless:true}) is supported in zod 4.3.6
notes: z.config is global; setting jitless also affects server parsing (no CSP there, harmless) and keeps a single intentional entry point.

findings.reason: Tools index claimed 12 species (actual 10), README described an uncalibrated planner, the accessibility statement overclaimed, and legacy blog claims were indexed without an evidence gate.

acceptance_results.criterion: Tool counts and summaries are derived from one source of truth.
acceptance_results.evidence: Species data extracted to src/data/tree-species.ts; SpeciesIdentifier imports it and tools/page.tsx derives the count via treeDatabase.length. Built /tools shows 'Browse 10 common Omaha-area trees'; tree-species.test pins the count and uniqueness.

acceptance_results.criterion: README accurately describes the current planner.
acceptance_results.evidence: README now states the cost output is a planning range built from published 2025 height-based pricing (src/data/tree-removal-pricing.ts) with modifiers, not a quote; version updated to 16.2.11.

acceptance_results.criterion: Accessibility language distinguishes goals, tested results, and known limitations.
acceptance_results.evidence: Per owner decision, the statement reframes practices as goals, notes automated per-build contrast/heading verification, presents AT compatibility as targets pending verification, and drops the unsupported 2-business-day SLA (now 'as promptly as we can'); Known Limitations retains the real-AT gate.

acceptance_results.criterion: Material legacy content claims have evidence or are revised, migrated, or withheld.
acceptance_results.evidence: Per owner decision, the legacy /blog and /blog/[slug] are set to robots noindex and removed from the sitemap (withheld from search) pending review/migration to the Treehouse evidence gate; built output shows 'noindex, follow' and zero /blog entries in sitemap.xml.

verification: manual read of README and accessibility page
notes: Legacy blog posts remain reachable by direct link; their content review/migration is a tracked owner follow-up.

findings.reason: Owner approved deleting the confirmed-dead files and unused dependencies; ripgrep confirmed no remaining consumers.

acceptance_results.criterion: Every removed file and package has no remaining consumer.
acceptance_results.evidence: Pre-deletion ripgrep found no external importers of the five files; @base-ui/react and class-variance-authority were only used by the deleted button.tsx and zustand had zero usage.

acceptance_results.criterion: npm ls, typecheck, tests, lint, and build pass after deletion.
acceptance_results.evidence: npm ls reports the three packages absent; typecheck clean, 46 unit tests pass, lint 0 warnings, build succeeds; @types/node aligned to ^22 for the Node 22 runtime.

acceptance_results.criterion: The configured dead-code check has no unexplained findings.
acceptance_results.evidence: npx knip after removal no longer lists any of the five files or three packages; remaining knip entries are pre-existing intentional data/config exports and test-only exports, explained and outside the approved deletion scope. A standing knip CI gate was deferred to avoid noise over those pre-existing exports.

notes: CLEANUP_POLICY.md archive log updated to record ContactForm.v1.tsx as discarded 2026-07-23 under CLEAN-001.

findings.reason: CI permitted warnings and lacked shell, console, metadata, accessibility, keyboard, phone-boundary, and cross-engine gates.

acceptance_results.criterion: Lint fails on any warning.
acceptance_results.evidence: lint script is 'eslint . --max-warnings=0' and CI runs npm run lint; a reintroduced warning fails the job.

acceptance_results.criterion: Representative routes have automated browser invariants and accessibility checks.
acceptance_results.evidence: New specs add axe accessibility scans (10 routes), shell-count + canonical/title + console-cleanliness invariants, and keyboard behavior; all run in CI e2e.

acceptance_results.criterion: Form boundary and failure recovery tests cover the shared validation contract.
acceptance_results.evidence: fields.test.ts covers 7/9/10-digit boundaries and the server superset; lead-form-boundary.spec drives the homepage form's 7-digit rejection in the browser.

acceptance_results.criterion: At least one focused smoke suite runs in Firefox and WebKit or the unsupported scope is documented.
acceptance_results.evidence: playwright.config adds firefox+webkit projects filtered to @smoke; CI installs all three engines. Firefox @smoke was verified locally (2/2). WebKit runs in CI; local WebKit needs host libs (playwright install --with-deps) unavailable in this sandbox, documented in the config comment.

acceptance_results.criterion: Dependency update automation is configured with controlled grouping.
acceptance_results.evidence: .github/dependabot.yml adds weekly npm updates grouped into production and dev minor/patch batches (majors separate) plus grouped github-actions updates.

notes: A standing dead-code (knip) CI gate is deferred (see CLEAN-001) to avoid noise over pre-existing intentional exports.

findings.reason: Owner approved marking this blocked; the retired-service-worker cleanup can only be removed after a production observation window that is unavailable in this session.

acceptance_results.criterion: Production clients no longer report the retired /sw.js registration or known caches during the agreed window.
acceptance_results.evidence: Production service-worker state cannot be observed from the local repository; no deployment observation window available.

acceptance_results.criterion: The cleanup component and migration-only styles are removed in the following release.
acceptance_results.evidence: Removal is gated on the production observation above; ServiceWorkerCleanup and migration-only styles are intentionally retained until then.

acceptance_results.criterion: A post-removal production smoke test passes.
acceptance_results.evidence: No removal performed, so no post-removal smoke test is applicable yet.

verification: source inspection confirms ServiceWorkerCleanup remains correctly scoped and mounted
notes: Temporary-migration lifecycle item; requires authorized production browser evidence before retirement.

findings.reason: The clean reproducible pipeline is a strength to preserve; it was kept green through every phase and strengthened by TEST-001.

acceptance_results.criterion: The clean install, typecheck, unit tests, build, and end-to-end suite remain green after each cleanup phase.
acceptance_results.evidence: After the full change set: typecheck clean, lint 0 warnings, 46 unit tests pass, webpack build succeeds, and 30 Chromium + 2 Firefox e2e pass. Unit count grew 36 to 46 and e2e added accessibility/shell/metadata/console/keyboard/boundary gates.

verification: npm run typecheck, lint, test, and build
notes: Baseline preserved and strengthened rather than degraded.

findings.reason: Strong homepage performance is a strength to preserve; no change in this revision degrades it.

acceptance_results.criterion: Representative mobile performance remains near the current baseline after the cleanup.
acceptance_results.evidence: No render-blocking or heavyweight client code was added; the production build still emits Static/SSG output for representative routes; removing the local Vercel scripts is net-positive and dead code was deleted. @axe-core/playwright is a devDependency only (not shipped).

verification: next build route table shows unchanged static/SSG rendering strategy
no new client-shipped dependencies added to the app bundle
notes: Production field Lighthouse comparison was not re-run in this session (no Lighthouse locally); recommended as a post-deploy confirmation.
